skill.name: devops-orchestrator
skill.description: Coordinates DevOps planning for CI/CD, infrastructure, deployments, monitoring, and operational standards.
skill.summary: Plan and coordinate DevOps workflows across pipelines, infrastructure, monitoring, and releases.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby or shell execution patterns, one weak cryptography pattern, and one system reconnaissance pattern. Review found these are Markdown examples, inline backtick labels, file-name text, and checklist text rather than executable code or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The 22 external command detections point to Markdown code fences, tree diagrams, YAML examples, JSON examples, and inline skill names. No executable script, shell invocation, Ruby code, user-controlled command, or network operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows Markdown examples and labels, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. The file contains only guidance text and no runnable code path.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is File Name Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alert at line 3 appears to match the text devops-standard.md in the skill description. No MD5, SHA1, encryption routine, hashing API, or credential handling is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is YAML front matter text and references a Markdown file name. There is no cryptographic operation on or near the line.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Checklist Text
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert at line 264 points to the Anti-Patterns to Avoid heading. Nearby lines list operational anti-patterns, not host discovery, environment probing, or command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a documentation heading and contains no recon command or instruction to inspect a host. The surrounding content is a safety checklist.

content.value_statement: DevOps work often spans pipelines, infrastructure, monitoring, and release controls. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured DevOps lead workflow.
actual_capabilities: Frames CI/CD pipeline tasks across build, test, deployment, and release management.
Guides infrastructure planning for containers, cloud resources, configuration, and environment provisioning.
Defines monitoring and observability checks for metrics, logs, alerts, and dashboards.
Describes deployment strategies including blue-green, canary, and rolling releases.
Uses DORA-style success metrics for deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, and failure rate.
limitations: Does not include executable automation scripts or provider-specific infrastructure modules.
Requires project-specific cloud, security, and deployment details from the user.
Does not replace human approval for production deployment or incident response.
Does not directly connect to CI systems, cloud accounts, or monitoring tools.

use_cases.title: Plan a Release Pipeline
use_cases.description: Create a structured CI/CD plan with build, test, security, deployment, rollback, and monitoring stages.

use_cases.title: Prepare Cloud Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Outline infrastructure needs for containers, databases, cache, scaling, backups, and disaster recovery.

use_cases.title: Improve Operational Readiness
use_cases.description: Review monitoring, alerting, runbooks, and DORA metrics before a production deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Pipeline Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-orchestrator skill to plan a CI/CD pipeline for this service. Include build, test, security scan, deploy, rollback, and monitoring steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review Deployment Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-orchestrator skill to review our deployment readiness. Check infrastructure, secrets, approvals, rollback, health checks, alerts, and runbooks.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Deployment Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-orchestrator skill to compare blue-green, canary, and rolling deployment for this service. Recommend one strategy with validation and rollback steps.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Operating Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the devops-orchestrator skill to design an operating model for deployments. Include CI/CD ownership, infrastructure standards, monitoring, DORA metrics, and incident handoffs.

output_examples.input: Plan a production deployment for a containerized API.
output: Pipeline stages for build, unit tests, integration tests, security scan, artifact creation, and deployment.
Infrastructure checklist covering replicas, resource limits, scaling, backups, and rollback capacity.
Monitoring checklist for availability, latency, error rate, throughput, saturation, and alert severity.

output_examples.input: Assess whether our release process is ready.
output: Readiness gaps grouped by pipeline, infrastructure, monitoring, security, and documentation.
Recommended deployment strategy with approval gates and rollback conditions.
DORA metrics to track after release, including deployment frequency, lead time, MTTR, and change failure rate.

best_practices: Provide environment, provider, service architecture, and release constraints before asking for a plan.
Ask for rollback, monitoring, and approval steps in every production deployment workflow.
Use the generated checklist as a review aid before making changes in real systems.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as an unattended production deployment agent.
Do not paste secrets, tokens, or private credentials into prompts.
Do not accept generic infrastructure advice without mapping it to your platform controls.

faq.question: Does this skill run deployments?
faq.answer: No. It provides DevOps planning and review guidance. It does not execute deployment commands.

faq.question: Can it help with CI/CD design?
faq.answer: Yes. It structures build, test, security scan, deployment, rollback, and release management steps.

faq.question: Does it support cloud infrastructure planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers cloud resources, containers, configuration, scaling, backup, and disaster recovery planning.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use it for DevOps reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. Claude Code and Codex can use it to organize DevOps tasks and review operational readiness.

faq.question: Does it include monitoring guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers application metrics, infrastructure metrics, logs, error tracking, alerts, and dashboards.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide service architecture, environment, cloud provider, pipeline constraints, security requirements, and release goals.
